rationale_for_baseline_tier: A steroid-dependent patient in decompensated shock — refractory hypotension (~85 systolic), tachycardia, low-grade fever and progressive altered mental status — from an evolving adrenal crisis precipitated by intercurrent illness and missed hydrocortisone. This is an immediate threat to life requiring parenteral steroid and IV fluids within minutes; T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) is the only appropriate tier. The emergency hydrocortisone injection should be given now if available; the ambulance provides IV steroid, fluid resuscitation and monitoring during transport.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Refractory hypotension / profound weakness with inability to stand and no steroid on board
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately and give the emergency hydrocortisone injection now if a kit is available
escalation_edges.rationale: Decompensated adrenal-crisis shock needs parenteral steroid and IV saline within minutes; oral treatment cannot reverse it.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This is an emergency — call 000 for an ambulance now and tell them it's an adrenal crisis. If you have his emergency hydrocortisone injection, give it into the outer thigh straight away while you wait.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening drowsiness/confusion, or loss of consciousness / stops breathing before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Stay on the line with 000; if he stops breathing, begin CPR on the dispatcher's instructions
escalation_edges.rationale: Progression to coma or arrest — CPR and paramedic resuscitation are the critical resource; lay him flat with legs raised while waiting.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he becomes unresponsive or stops breathing normally, tell 000 immediately, lay him flat, and start chest compressions following the dispatcher's instructions.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Continued vomiting so no oral steroid can be absorbed
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Do not wait or retry tablets — parenteral steroid via injection/ambulance is required now
escalation_edges.rationale: Vomiting removes the oral route; the definitive treatment must be given parenterally without delay.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because he can't keep tablets down, don't keep trying them — he needs the injection and the ambulance now.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Any intercurrent illness in this patient mandates a very low threshold for stress dosing and emergency injection; the crisis must be assumed and treated, not waited out.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Spouse present but frightened and untrained in giving the emergency injection
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear, calm step-by-step instructions for the injection kit if available and keep 000 on the line; reassure that the injection through the outer thigh is safe to give and that paramedics are en route.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and say it is an adrenal crisis in a person with Addison's disease. If you have his emergency hydrocortisone injection kit, give the injection into the middle of his outer thigh straight away. Keep him lying flat with his legs raised; do not let him sit up or stand. Do not give more tablets or fluids by mouth while he is vomiting and drowsy. Stay with him and unlock the door for the paramedics.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic or in-hours care.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary; ensure the endocrinologist reviews the sick-day plan, confirms an in-date emergency hydrocortisone injection kit is supplied, and that the carer is trained to use it.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Mislabelling the presentation as simple gastroenteritis/dehydration and advising home management, oral fluids or a routine GP review.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Untreated adrenal crisis progresses to irreversible hypovolaemic shock, coma and death within hours.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising acuity but directing to urgent care / same-day GP rather than an emergency ambulance, delaying parenteral steroid and IV resuscitation.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: T5 is correct here, so over-triage is not a meaningful risk; calling 000 for this presentation is always appropriate.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Concurrent infection (source of the fever) cannot be characterised remotely, and the home blood-pressure reading is unverified.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the adrenal crisis presumptively and escalate immediately; the uncertainty about the infective source is resolved in hospital and never delays steroid or the ambulance.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Waiting for confirmation of the diagnosis or the infection source, or discounting the crisis because the vitals are self-reported, before escalating.
